introSection: Oklahoma Land Run was a wooden roller coaster located in Jeremy Reid's Backyard in Newcastle, Oklahoma, United States. After building the coaster, Reid went on to work at Arrow Dynamics for a short time.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
In 1997, Jeremy Reid built a simple U-shuttle coaster in his parents' backyard, the following year he would extend the track, turning the spike into a hill, and adding a banked turn.

Reid added a chain lift hill and some ground-supported track in 1999, completing the coaster's circuit.

In 2007, Reid relocated the chain lift and built an elevated turn following it, giving the ride an oval-shaped layout.

The coaster was demolished 2016 or earlier.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: It was tall and had a top speed of. The track was made of pressure-treated southern yellow pine, with polyethylene strips as running rails.

fullText:
Oklahoma Land Run was a wooden roller coaster located in Jeremy Reid's Backyard in Newcastle, Oklahoma, United States. After building the coaster, Reid went on to work at Arrow Dynamics for a short time.

In 1997, Jeremy Reid built a simple U-shuttle coaster in his parents' backyard, the following year he would extend the track, turning the spike into a hill, and adding a banked turn.

Reid added a chain lift hill and some ground-supported track in 1999, completing the coaster's circuit.

In 2007, Reid relocated the chain lift and built an elevated turn following it, giving the ride an oval-shaped layout.

The coaster was demolished 2016 or earlier.

It was tall and had a top speed of. The track was made of pressure-treated southern yellow pine, with polyethylene strips as running rails.
